api: Ticket Tailor REST API v1 (https://app.tickettailor-stitching.com/openapi.yml, linked from https://developers.tickettailor.com/)
scope: Pass B full-surface expansion against the published OpenAPI spec (fetched 2026-07-03 via the Docusaurus site's own openapi-docs plugin config, api.specPath = https://app.tickettailor-stitching.com/openapi.yml). IMPORTANT DISCOVERY: the real API's actual pagination convention for every list endpoint -- including the 3 original legacy-parity streams (events/orders/issued_tickets) -- is cursor-based (starting_after/ending_before/limit query params, a data[]/links{next,previous} envelope), NOT the page/limit page-number convention the legacy connector and the wave2 bundle both used; page/limit is not a documented parameter on any Ticket Tailor list endpoint and would silently never advance past page 1 against the live API (the real cursor param, starting_after, was simply never sent). This was a genuine functional defect (never a deliberately documented parity decision, see docs.md), not a Pass B scope choice, so it is FIXED in this pass: all list streams (original 3 plus every new one) now declare pagination.type=next_url reading links.next (a same-host RELATIVE path+query string per Ticket Tailor's own documented example, e.g. '/v1/events?starting_after=ev_123' -- allow_cross_host:true is required purely because the value has no scheme+host at all, not because it targets a different host; see docs.md). 17 read streams total (3 original plus 14 new: event_series, holds, discounts, membership_types, issued_memberships, products, stores, vouchers, checkout_forms, voucher_codes, checkout_form_elements, event_series_overrides, event_series_waitlist_signups, overview) and 25 write actions covering event-series/hold/discount/membership/voucher/product lifecycle, ticket issuance/voiding/check-in, and order payment confirmation. Excluded: single-object detail endpoints duplicating an already-covered list stream, ticket-type/ticket-group/bundle/override sub-resource mutations under event_series (breadth-vs-cost triage, matching bitly's precedent for narrower nested-configuration sub-resources), checkout-form-element updates, store updates, membership photo-share lookup, ping/overview non-list utility endpoints (overview IS covered as a single-object stream; ping is excluded), and 2 writes with a bare-JSON-nested-object-in-a-form-body ambiguity this dialect cannot verify (create_hold/update_hold's ticket_type_id field).

excluded.reason: single-event detail; identical record shape to the events list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-order detail; identical record shape to the orders list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-issued-ticket detail; identical record shape to the issued_tickets list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

a raw check-in event log rather than a stable entity list (each check-in is a point-in-time action against an issued ticket already fully represented by the issued_tickets stream's own checked_in field); Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single-event-series detail; identical record shape to the event_series list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream

event occurrences of a series are already reachable through the top-level events stream (each occurrence carries its own id/dates); adding a second, series-scoped fan_out view of the identical record shape would be a duplicate of intent, not a distinct resource

creates a new occurrence within an event series; narrower event-scheduling sub-resource mutation, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage (event_series' own create/update/status writes cover the primary lifecycle)
single-occurrence detail; same record shape as the top-level events stream's per-item object
updates a single event occurrence's schedule; narrower sub-resource mutation, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
deletes a single event occurrence; narrower sub-resource mutation, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
creates a schedule override for an event series; narrower sub-resource configuration mutation, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
updates a schedule override; same narrow sub-resource mutation reasoning as the override create above
removes a single waitlist signup; narrow sub-resource mutation, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
creates a ticket bundle configuration; narrow event-configuration sub-resource mutation with no corresponding read stream to round-trip against, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
updates a ticket bundle configuration; same narrow sub-resource reasoning as bundle create above
deletes a ticket bundle configuration; same narrow sub-resource reasoning as bundle create above
creates a ticket-type grouping configuration; narrow event-configuration sub-resource mutation with no corresponding read stream, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
updates a ticket-group configuration; same narrow sub-resource reasoning as ticket-group create above
deletes a ticket-group configuration; same narrow sub-resource reasoning as ticket-group create above
creates a ticket type configuration; narrow event-configuration sub-resource mutation with no corresponding read stream, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
updates a ticket type configuration; same narrow sub-resource reasoning as ticket-type create above
deletes a ticket type configuration; same narrow sub-resource reasoning as ticket-type create above

ENGINE_GAP: the documented request body's required ticket_type_id field is a JSON object (additionalProperties, e.g. {tt_1: 1, tt_2: 5}) sent inside an application/x-www-form-urlencoded body -- this dialect's form-body construction (engine/write.go buildForm/stringifyAny) can only stringify a nested object value as a single JSON-string-valued form field, not the bracket-notation (ticket_type_id[tt_1]=1) or repeated-key form encoding a nested object in a form body typically requires; unverifiable which encoding Ticket Tailor's real endpoint expects without a live test, so not implemented rather than guessed
single-hold detail; identical record shape to the holds list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
ENGINE_GAP: same nested-object-in-form-body ambiguity as POST /v1/holds above (this endpoint's ticket_type_id field is optional but carries the identical encoding ambiguity whenever it is set)
single-discount detail; identical record shape to the discounts list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-membership-type detail; identical record shape to the membership_types list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
updates a membership type's validity/conditions configuration; narrow sub-resource mutation, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage (create/delete already cover the primary lifecycle)
single-issued-membership detail; identical record shape to the issued_memberships list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
records a membership redemption at check-in time for a specific event; narrow operational sub-workflow distinct from the membership lifecycle writes already covered, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
returns a single photo-URL lookup by issued-membership code, not a syncable object stream
single-product detail; identical record shape to the products list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-store detail; identical record shape to the stores list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
updates store-level currency/product configuration; a single-tenant configuration endpoint (most box offices have exactly one store) with limited reverse-ETL value, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
single-voucher detail; identical record shape to the vouchers list stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
single-checkout-form-element detail; identical record shape to the checkout_form_elements fan_out stream's per-item object, already covered by that stream
updates a single checkout-form question/element's configuration; narrow sub-resource mutation, Pass B breadth-vs-cost triage
a liveness/health-check endpoint returning a static {version} payload, not a syncable object stream
